title: Pathophysiology of Asthma
description: Composite figure showing lung and airway location plus normal airway cross-section versus narrowed asthmatic airway with mucus, inflammation, and smooth muscle tightening.
caption: Figure A displays the location of the lungs and airways in the body. Figure B shows a cross section of a normal airway. Figure C illustrates a cross section of an airway during asthma symptoms.

sourceReference.fileName: Pathophysiology Of Asthma _ Treatment & Management _ Point of Care - StatPearls.pdf

title: Chest radiograph depicting asthma
description: Anterior and lateral chest radiographs of a patient with asthma highlighting pneumomediastinum and increased bronchovascular markings.
caption: A Chest Radiograph Depicting Asthma. The image depicts both anterior and lateral radiographs of a patient with asthma. The image highlights the presence of pneumomediastinum and increased bronchovascular markings.

sourceReference.fileName: Pathophysiology Of Asthma _ Treatment & Management _ Point of Care - StatPearls.pdf

title: Allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis on CT
description: Computed tomography image showing bronchiectasis in both upper lobes of a patient with bronchial asthma, useful as a complication/association resource.
caption: Allergic Bronchopulmonary Aspergillosis on Computed Tomography. Images reveal bronchiectasis in both upper lobes of a patient with bronchial asthma, indicative of allergic bronchopulmonary aspergillosis.

sourceReference.fileName: Pathophysiology Of Asthma _ Treatment & Management _ Point of Care - StatPearls.pdf

title: Asthma severity classification by National Asthma Education and Prevention Program
description: Severity classification table summarizing intermittent, mild, moderate, and severe asthma across symptom burden, night waking, activity limitation, and lung function.
caption: Asthma Severity Classification by The National Asthma Education and Prevention Program.

sourceReference.fileName: Pathophysiology Of Asthma _ Treatment & Management _ Point of Care - StatPearls.pdf

title: Cellular pathophysiology of asthma
description: Figure contrasting normal and asthmatic airway and mapping mediator sources to bronchoconstriction, mucus secretion, inflammatory cell infiltration, and epithelial damage.
caption: Cellular pathophysiology of asthma. Top, cross-section of the normal airway and the asthmatic airway. Bottom, the inflammatory cells and neurotransmitters implicated in asthma pathophysiology.

title: Gaseous exchange in the lung
description: Normal respiratory reference diagram showing oxygen movement into blood and carbon dioxide removal across the alveolar-capillary bed; useful as baseline comparison for asthma physiology modules.
caption: Gaseous Exchange in the Lung. The image illustrates gas exchange in the lungs, showing the movement of oxygen into the bloodstream and carbon dioxide expelled through the alveolar-capillary bed.
